# services.kanidm

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by scripts/nix-options.sh. Do not edit by hand. -->

All options under `services.kanidm`.

| Option | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `services.kanidm.client.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the Kanidm client. |
| `services.kanidm.client.settings` | `open submodule of (TOML value)` | Configure Kanidm clients, needed for the PAM daemon. See [the documentation](https://kanidm.github.io/kanidm/stable/client_tools.html#kanidm-configuration) and [example configuration](https://github.com/kanidm/kanidm/blob/master/examples/config) for possible values. |
| `services.kanidm.client.settings.uri` | `string` | Address of the Kanidm server. |
| `services.kanidm.package` | `package` | The kanidm package to use. Must be set to an explicit version. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.acceptInvalidCerts` | `boolean` | Whether to allow invalid certificates when provisioning the target instance. By default this is only allowed when the instanceUrl is localhost. This is dangerous when used with an external URL. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.adminPasswordFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to a file containing the admin password for kanidm. Do NOT use a file from the nix store here! |
| `services.kanidm.provision.autoRemove` | `boolean` | Determines whether deleting an entity in this provisioning config should automatically cause them to be removed from kanidm, too. This works because the provisioning tool tracks all entities it has ever created. If this is set to false, you need to explicitly specify `present = false` to delete an entity. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable provisioning of groups, users and oauth2 resource servers. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.extraJsonFile` | `null or absolute path` | A JSON file for provisioning persons, groups & systems. Options set in this file take precedence over values set using the other options. The files get deeply merged, and deduplicated. The accepted JSON schema can be found at <https://github.com/oddlama/kanidm-provision#json-schema>. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.groups` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Provisioning of kanidm groups |
| `services.kanidm.provision.groups.<name>.members` | `list of string` | List of kanidm entities (persons, groups, ...) which are part of this group. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.groups.<name>.overwriteMembers` | `boolean` | Whether the member list should be overwritten each time (true) or appended (false). Append mode allows interactive group management in addition to the declared members. Also, future member removals cannot be reflected automatically in append mode. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.groups.<name>.present` | `boolean` | Whether to ensure that this group is present or absent. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.idmAdminPasswordFile` | `null or absolute path` | Path to a file containing the idm admin password for kanidm. Do NOT use a file from the nix store here! If this is not given but provisioning is enabled, the idm_admin password will be reset on each restart. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.instanceUrl` | `string` | The instance url to which the provisioning tool should connect. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.persons` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Provisioning of kanidm persons |
| `services.kanidm.provision.persons.<name>.displayName` | `string` | Display name |
| `services.kanidm.provision.persons.<name>.groups` | `list of string` | List of groups this person should belong to. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.persons.<name>.legalName` | `null or string` | Full legal name |
| `services.kanidm.provision.persons.<name>.mailAddresses` | `list of string` | Mail addresses. First given address is considered the primary address. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.persons.<name>.present` | `boolean` | Whether to ensure that this person is present or absent. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Provisioning of oauth2 resource servers |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.allowInsecureClientDisablePkce` | `boolean` | Disable PKCE on this oauth2 resource server to work around insecure clients that may not support it. You should request the client to enable PKCE! Only for non-public clients. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.basicSecretFile` | `null or absolute path` | The basic secret to use for this service. If null, the random secret generated by kanidm will not be touched. Do NOT use a path from the nix store here! |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.claimMaps` | `attribute set of (submodule)` | Adds additional claims (and values) based on which kanidm groups an authenticating party belongs to. See [Claim Maps](https://kanidm.github.io/kanidm/master/integrations/oauth2.html#custom-claim-maps) for more information. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.claimMaps.<name>.joinType` | `one of "array", "csv", "ssv"` | Determines how multiple values are joined to create the claim value. See [Claim Maps](https://kanidm.github.io/kanidm/master/integrations/oauth2.html#custom-claim-maps) for more information. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.claimMaps.<name>.valuesByGroup` | `attribute set of list of string` | Maps kanidm groups to values for the claim. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.displayName` | `string` | Display name |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.enableLegacyCrypto` | `boolean` | Enable legacy crypto on this client. Allows JWT signing algorthms like RS256. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.enableLocalhostRedirects` | `boolean` | Allow localhost redirects. Only for public clients. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.imageFile` | `null or absolute path` | Application image to display in the WebUI. Kanidm supports "image/jpeg", "image/png", "image/gif", "image/svg+xml", and "image/webp". The image will be uploaded each time kanidm-provision is run. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.originLanding` | `string` | When redirecting from the Kanidm Apps Listing page, some linked applications may need to land on a specific page to trigger oauth2/oidc interactions. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.originUrl` | `string matching the pattern .*://?.*$ or non-empty (list of string matching the pattern .*://?.*$)` | The redirect URL of the service. These need to exactly match the OAuth2 redirect target |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.preferShortUsername` | `boolean` | Use 'name' instead of 'spn' in the preferred_username claim |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.present` | `boolean` | Whether to ensure that this oauth2 resource server is present or absent. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.public` | `boolean` | Whether this is a public client (enforces PKCE, doesn't use a basic secret) |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.removeOrphanedClaimMaps` | `boolean` | Whether claim maps not specified here but present in kanidm should be removed from kanidm. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.scopeMaps` | `attribute set of list of string` | Maps kanidm groups to returned oauth scopes. See [Scope Relations](https://kanidm.github.io/kanidm/stable/integrations/oauth2.html#scope-relationships) for more information. |
| `services.kanidm.provision.systems.oauth2.<name>.supplementaryScopeMaps` | `attribute set of list of string` | Maps kanidm groups to additionally returned oauth scopes. See [Scope Relations](https://kanidm.github.io/kanidm/stable/integrations/oauth2.html#scope-relationships) for more information. |
| `services.kanidm.server.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the Kanidm server. |
| `services.kanidm.server.settings` | `open submodule of (TOML value)` | Settings for Kanidm, see [the documentation](https://kanidm.github.io/kanidm/stable/server_configuration.html) and [example configuration](https://github.com/kanidm/kanidm/blob/master/examples/server.toml) for possible values. |
| `services.kanidm.server.settings.bindaddress` | `string` | Address/port combination the webserver binds to. |
| `services.kanidm.server.settings.db_path` | `absolute path` | Path to Kanidm database. |
| `services.kanidm.server.settings.domain` | `null or string` | The `domain` that Kanidm manages. Must be below or equal to the domain specified in `server.settings.origin`. This can be left at `null`, only if your instance has the role `ReadOnlyReplica`. While it is possible to change the domain later on, it requires extra steps! Please consider the warnings and execute the steps described [in the documentation](https://kanidm.github.io/kanidm/stable/administrivia.html#rename-the-domain). |
| `services.kanidm.server.settings.ldapbindaddress` | `null or string` | Address and port the LDAP server is bound to. Setting this to `null` disables the LDAP interface. |
| `services.kanidm.server.settings.log_level` | `one of "info", "debug", "trace"` | Log level of the server. |
| `services.kanidm.server.settings.online_backup.path` | `absolute path` | Path to the output directory for backups. |
| `services.kanidm.server.settings.online_backup.schedule` | `string` | The schedule for backups in cron format. |
| `services.kanidm.server.settings.online_backup.versions` | `unsigned integer, meaning >=0` | Number of backups to keep. The default is set to `0`, in order to disable backups by default. |
| `services.kanidm.server.settings.origin` | `string matching the pattern ^https://.*` | The origin of your Kanidm instance. Must have https as protocol. |
| `services.kanidm.server.settings.role` | `one of "WriteReplica", "WriteReplicaNoUI", "ReadOnlyReplica"` | The role of this server. This affects the replication relationship and thereby available features. |
| `services.kanidm.server.settings.tls_chain` | `absolute path` | TLS chain in pem format. |
| `services.kanidm.server.settings.tls_key` | `absolute path` | TLS key in pem format. |
| `services.kanidm.unix.enable` | `boolean` | Whether to enable the Kanidm PAM and NSS integration. |
| `services.kanidm.unix.settings` | `open submodule of (TOML value)` | Configure Kanidm unix daemon. See [the documentation](https://kanidm.github.io/kanidm/stable/integrations/pam_and_nsswitch.html#the-unix-daemon) and [example configuration](https://github.com/kanidm/kanidm/blob/master/examples/unixd) for possible values. |
| `services.kanidm.unix.settings.hsm_pin_path` | `absolute path` | Path to a HSM pin. |
| `services.kanidm.unix.settings.kanidm.pam_allowed_login_groups` | `list of string` | Kanidm groups that are allowed to login using PAM. |
| `services.kanidm.unix.sshIntegration` | `boolean` | Whether to enable Kanidm SSH keys login. |
